Home › Lifestyle Medicine
The Main Line's Only In-Person Option

Lifestyle Medicine Wayne PA

What is Lifestyle Medicine, and why does it matter for your primary care?

Lifestyle medicine is an evidence-based clinical specialty that uses therapeutic changes in nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, social connection, and avoidance of harmful substances to prevent, treat, and even reverse chronic disease.

At Forward Family Medicine, you don't have to seek this out separately. It's integrated into every visit — because your doctor, Dr. Philip Lieberman, is board-certified in it.

Schedule a Free Meet & Greet
Dr. Philip Lieberman, MD Dual Board-Certified Physician
  • Board-Certified: Lifestyle Medicine
  • Board-Certified: Family Medicine
  • Culinary Arts Degree
  • Nutrition Science Degree
  • Thomas Jefferson University MD
  • U.S. Navy Physician
  • Built LM Residency Curriculum at Abington
Why It Matters

We Are the Only Practice on the Main Line With This

No other local DPC practice offers it at all. At Forward Family Medicine, it's in-person, it's board-certified, and it's integrated into every visit at no extra charge.

🏅 Dual Board-Certified Family Medicine + Lifestyle Medicine
🍽️ Culinary Trained Real food knowledge, not textbook advice
📋 Built the Curriculum Trained other physicians at Abington
✅ Included in Membership No extra fees, no separate appointments
What We Offer

What's Included in Your Lifestyle Medicine Care

Your lifestyle medicine care at Forward Family Medicine is built around your specific health history, your labs, your goals, and — critically — your actual life. Dr. Lieberman doesn't hand you a pamphlet. He works with you on a real, practical plan that accounts for what you eat, how you sleep, what stresses you, and what your body needs.

Because he trained in culinary arts before medical school, his nutrition guidance is specific, practical, and grounded in food that actually tastes good. That's rare in medicine.

  • Comprehensive lifestyle assessment at intake and regularly throughout care
  • Personalized nutrition guidance — culinary-informed, practical, real food
  • Physical activity planning tailored to your fitness level and preferences
  • Sleep optimization protocols based on your specific patterns
  • Stress and mental wellness support
  • Evidence-based protocols for type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, obesity
  • Chronic disease management through lifestyle intervention — alongside or instead of medication
  • Ongoing accountability built into your regular appointments
Who Benefits

Lifestyle Medicine Is For You If…

💊

You Have a Chronic Condition

Type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, high cholesterol, obesity, or metabolic syndrome — lifestyle medicine has the strongest evidence base for all of them.

🛡️

You Want to Stay Healthy

Prevention is the most powerful application of lifestyle medicine. Build a foundation now so that chronic disease doesn't become your story later.

⚡

You Want More Energy

Sleep, nutrition, movement, and stress management are the levers. Dr. Lieberman knows how to use them — and how to fit them into your real life.

🔄

You've Tried Before and It Didn't Stick

Generic advice doesn't work. A culinary-trained, board-certified physician who builds a plan around your specific situation — that's different.

Dr. Lieberman's Story

A Different Kind of Medical Training

Most doctors learn about nutrition in a single semester. Dr. Lieberman went to culinary school first. Then nutrition science. Then Jefferson Medical College. Then the Navy. Then he wrote the curriculum that teaches other physicians how to practice lifestyle medicine.

The result is a doctor who can sit with you in the exam room, interpret your labs, and then help you figure out exactly what to put on your plate — and how to make it taste good.

Dr. Lieberman's Path

Culinary Arts Degree Learning food, technique, and flavor from the ground up
BS in Nutrition Science The clinical science of food, metabolism, and health
MD — Thomas Jefferson University Medical training grounded in an already-deep nutritional foundation
U.S. Navy Physician Primary care practice in a demanding environment
Dual Board Certification Family Medicine + Lifestyle Medicine at Abington
Built the LM Residency Curriculum Enabled Abington residents to earn their own Lifestyle Medicine board certification
FAQ

Lifestyle Medicine FAQs

Lifestyle medicine is an evidence-based clinical specialty that uses therapeutic changes in nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, social connection, and avoidance of harmful substances to prevent, treat, and even reverse chronic disease. Dr. Lieberman is board-certified in it by the American College of Lifestyle Medicine.

Yes. Lifestyle medicine is fully integrated into Dr. Lieberman's practice — not a separate fee or add-on service. It's part of how he practices with every patient.

Not necessarily — changes happen based on your progress and your preferences. Many patients are able to reduce or eliminate certain medications as their health improves, but this is always a collaborative, evidence-based process. Your safety and comfort come first.

No. Lifestyle medicine is equally powerful for prevention — helping healthy patients stay healthy, optimize their energy, and build a foundation for long-term wellness. You don't have to be sick to benefit from better nutrition, sleep, and stress management.

Most primary care physicians receive very limited nutrition and lifestyle training in medical school. Dr. Lieberman earned degrees in culinary arts and nutrition science before medical school, is board-certified in Lifestyle Medicine, and developed a formal LM curriculum for other physicians. The depth of his training is genuinely different.

Lifestyle medicine has the strongest evidence base for type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, obesity, metabolic syndrome, sleep disorders, anxiety, and depression. It also plays a powerful role in cancer prevention and overall longevity.

Go Deeper

Lifestyle Medicine Library

Browse our evidence-based article library covering nutrition, physical activity, sleep, metabolic health, stress management, and cardiovascular prevention — all through a lifestyle medicine lens.

Schedule a free meet and greet with Dr. Lieberman. Come in, ask everything, and see for yourself what in-person, board-certified lifestyle medicine actually looks like.
